meta.title: Meeting room booking portal
meta.notes: Synthetic fixture used by the planning-engine parity test. It is deliberately small but not trivial: several epics, dev tasks spread across every layer, and days that do not divide evenly into whole working days.

tasks.description: Stores the bookable rooms with their capacity, floor and fixed equipment.

dev_tasks.description: Tables room, equipment and room_equipment, with the seed data for the two pilot buildings.
Paginated list with filters on building, floor and capacity.
Opening hours per room, blackout windows and the minimum notice before a booking.

tasks.description: Lets a facility manager correct the data of a single room.

dev_tasks.description: Read and edit view with validation on capacity and opening hours.
Records who changed what and when, shown as a table under the form.

e2e.label: E2E test of the Rooms and resources flow

tasks.description: The weekly grid a colleague uses to find a free slot and take it.

dev_tasks.description: Tables booking and booking_attendee, with the uniqueness constraint on room plus time slot.
Week grid by room, with drag to select a slot and the room filter kept in the URL.
Rejects a booking that overlaps an existing one on the same room, including the setup buffer between meetings.
Sends the invitation to the attendees and keeps it in step when the booking moves.

tasks.description: Frees a slot up again, warning the people who were expected.

dev_tasks.description: Cancel action with a reason, permitted only to the organiser or a facility manager.
Notification on cancellation and on a change of time or room.

e2e.label: E2E test of the Booking flow

tasks.description: Shows how much each room is actually used, so the unused ones can be given up.

dev_tasks.description: Hours booked and hours actually used per room and per month, materialised nightly.
Table plus bar chart, with a period filter and a CSV export.

e2e.label: E2E test of the Reporting flow

assumptions: The list of rooms is loaded once at go-live and maintained by hand afterwards: no sync with the facilities system.
Attendees are looked up in the company directory, which already exposes a search endpoint.
A booking involves one room only: splitting a meeting across two rooms is out of scope.
considerations: The weekly calendar is the heaviest single item of the estimate and the one the users will judge the tool by.
The overlap check and the setup buffer between meetings are the two rules most likely to change after the first demo.

open_questions.question: Can a booking be made on behalf of someone else, or only for oneself?
open_questions.estimated_impact: +1 day if delegation has to be handled

open_questions.question: Does actual usage come from the badge readers or from a check-in in the app?
open_questions.estimated_impact: +2 days if the badge readers have to be integrated
